title: MySQL DDL Parsing Flow
interaction_flow: MySqlDdlParserPerf.antlr(ParserState) → ParserState.antlrParser.parse(ddl, tables)
AntlrDdlParser.parse(ddlContent, Tables): CharStreams.fromString(ddlContent) → CodePointCharStream
AntlrDdlParser → MySqlAntlrDdlParser.createNewLexerInstance(CaseChangingCharStream) → new MySqlLexer(charStream)
AntlrDdlParser → new CommonTokenStream(lexer) [buffers all tokens from lexer]
AntlrDdlParser → MySqlAntlrDdlParser.createNewParserInstance(CommonTokenStream) → new MySqlParser(tokenStream)
AntlrDdlParser: parser.removeErrorListener(ConsoleErrorListener); parser.addErrorListener(ParsingErrorListener)
AntlrDdlParser → MySqlAntlrDdlParser.parseTree(MySqlParser) → parser.root() → returns ParseTree (AST)
AntlrDdlParser [if no parse errors] → MySqlAntlrDdlParser.createParseTreeWalkerListener() → new MySqlAntlrDdlParserListener(parser)

MySqlAntlrDdlParserListener constructor registers sub-listeners: CreateTableParserListener, AlterTableParserListener, DropTableParserListener, RenameTableParserListener, TruncateTableParserListener, CreateAndAlterDatabaseParserListener, DropDatabaseParserListener, CreateViewParserListener, AlterViewParserListener, DropViewParserListener, CreateUniqueIndexParserListener, SetStatementParserListener, UseStatementParserListener
AntlrDdlParser → ParseTreeWalker.DEFAULT.walk(MySqlAntlrDdlParserListener, parseTree) [AST traversal]
ParseTreeWalker → MySqlAntlrDdlParserListener: fires enterXxx/exitXxx callbacks, delegates to sub-listeners via ProxyParseTreeListenerUtil

CreateTableParserListener.exitColumnCreateTable(ctx): builds Table via tableEditor, calls parser.databaseTables().overwriteTable(tableEditor.create()), then parser.signalCreateTable(tableId, ctx) → adds TableCreatedEvent to DdlChanges

AlterTableParserListener.exitAlterTable(ctx): calls parser.databaseTables().overwriteTable(tableEditor.create()), then parser.signalAlterTable(tableId, null, ctx.getParent()) → adds TableAlteredEvent to DdlChanges
DropTableParserListener.exitDropTable(ctx): calls parser.databaseTables().removeTable(tableId), then parser.signalDropTable(tableId, statement) → adds TableDroppedEvent to DdlChanges
AntlrDdlParser.parse() returns getAndResetDdlChanges() (DdlChanges)

BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseDdl(partition, ddlStatements, databaseName, offset, sourceTime, snapshot): ddlParser.setCurrentSchema(databaseName), then ddlChanges = ddlParser.parse(ddlStatements, tables())
BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseDdl: ddlChanges.getEventsByDatabase(callback) — iterates events grouped by database
BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseDdl per event: handleTableEvent(offset, dbName, tableIds, sourceTime) → offset.tableEvent()
BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseDdl per event: emitChangeEvent(partition, offset, schemaChangeEvents, dbName, event, tableId, type, snapshot) → builds SchemaChangeEvent, adds to list
BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseDdl returns List<SchemaChangeEvent>
BinlogDatabaseSchema.applySchemaChange(SchemaChangeEvent): CREATE/ALTER → schemaChange.getTableChanges().forEach(x → buildAndRegisterSchema(x.getTable())); DROP → removeSchema(x.getId())
BinlogDatabaseSchema.applySchemaChange: record(schemaChange, tableChanges) [persists DDL to schema history store]

entry_points.benchmark: MySqlDdlParserPerf.antlr(ParserState) — JMH @Benchmark, Mode.AverageTime, measures parse() call per column count (1/2/5/10/20/50)
entry_points.snapshot: BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseSnapshotDdl(partition, ddl, db, offset, time) → parseDdl(..., snapshot=true)
entry_points.streaming: BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseStreamingDdl(partition, ddl, db, offset, time) → parseDdl(..., snapshot=false)

key_relationships: MySqlDdlParserPerf uses ParserState (inner @State(Scope.Thread) class) for JMH setup
ParserState holds: AbstractDdlParser antlrParser (MySqlAntlrDdlParser), Tables tables, String ddl
MySqlAntlrDdlParser extends AntlrDdlParser<MySqlLexer, MySqlParser> extends AbstractDdlParser
BinlogDatabaseSchema extends HistorizedRelationalDatabaseSchema; holds DdlParser ddlParser = MySqlAntlrDdlParser (created via abstract createDdlParser())
MySqlAntlrDdlParserListener extends MySqlParserBaseListener implements AntlrDdlParserListener; delegates to 13 sub-listeners
DdlChanges accumulates DdlParserListener.Event objects (TableCreatedEvent, TableAlteredEvent, TableDroppedEvent, SetVariableEvent, etc.) during parse
TokenStream (debezium-util) is a legacy text-level tokenization utility (Tokenizer + Token + cursor API: start/hasNext/consume); used by non-ANTLR parsers, not in this ANTLR flow directly
data_flow: Input: raw DDL String (e.g. 'CREATE TABLE t1 (id int primary key, v0 int)')
Step 1 — Lexer: DDL String → CodePointCharStream → CaseChangingCharStream (uppercased) → MySqlLexer → token sequence
Step 2 — Parser: token sequence → CommonTokenStream → MySqlParser.root() → ParseTree (ANTLR AST)
Step 3 — Listener walk: ParseTree → ParseTreeWalker.walk → MySqlAntlrDdlParserListener → sub-listeners update Tables in-memory model + signal events into DdlChanges
Step 4 — Schema events: DdlChanges → BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseDdl groups by database → List<SchemaChangeEvent>
Step 5 — Schema apply: SchemaChangeEvent → buildAndRegisterSchema / removeSchema + record() to schema history
error_handling: ParsingErrorListener captures ANTLR syntax errors during parsing; throws ParsingException or MultipleParsingExceptions
AntlrDdlParserListener collects walk errors; if throwErrorsFromTreeWalk=true, throws after walk
BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseDdl catches ParsingException/MultipleParsingExceptions; if skipUnparseableDdlStatements() logs warning else rethrows
MySqlAntlrDdlParserListener skips BEGIN...END blocks (skipNodes flag + skippedNodesCount counter)
important_notes: MySqlDdlParserPerf is a JMH microbenchmark comparing ANTLR parser performance across column counts (no legacy parser in current code). BinlogDatabaseSchema is abstract; concrete impls (MySqlDatabaseSchema, MariaDbDatabaseSchema) provide createDdlParser(). MySqlAntlrDdlParser uses BinlogSystemVariables for charset/collation resolution. Tables object is the mutable in-memory schema store updated directly by sub-listeners before signaling events. DdlChanges is returned from parse() and drives SchemaChangeEvent generation in BinlogDatabaseSchema.
